serving.natural_language_portion: A standard single-serve can of Coke Zero (330 ml / approximately 330 g) as typically served at a Korean restaurant in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, carbonated, cola, with caffeine, no sugar — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174851)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FDC SR Legacy entry 174851 'Beverages, carbonated, cola, with caffeine, no sugar' is the closest exact match for a zero-sugar cola beverage. Coca-Cola Zero Sugar branded entry (FDC ID 2346009) in USDA Branded Foods corroborates the zero-calorie, zero-sugar, zero-fat, zero-protein profile. Values per 100 g used.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: Coca-Cola Zero Sugar contains no glycemic carbohydrates (0 g sugar, 0 g total carbohydrate per 100 g). Zero-sugar cola beverages are not assigned a measurable GI because they produce no postprandial glucose response. GI is set to 1 (lowest valid positive sentinel) per Veritas convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages; gi_category is 'low'. Glycemic load per 330 ml serving = 0.0. Reference: Atkinson FS et al. (2021) confirms diet cola beverages have negligible GI.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (6 mg per 100 g; approximately 20 mg per 330 ml can)
hypertension.guidance: Coke Zero contains negligible sodium (~20 mg per 330 ml can) and no added sugar, making it a low-concern beverage for hypertension; however, regular consumption of caffeinated beverages may cause transient blood pressure elevation and should be moderated in hypertensive individuals.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero glycemic carbohydrates; sweetened with aspartame and acesulfame-K
type2_diabetes.guidance: Coke Zero contains 0 g sugar and 0 g total carbohydrate per serving, producing no acute postprandial glucose rise; it is generally considered acceptable for people with Type 2 Diabetes as an occasional alternative to sugar-sweetened beverages, though long-term effects of artificial sweeteners on insulin sensitivity remain under research review.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero sugar, trace sodium; caffeine content (~34 mg per 330 ml)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Coke Zero contains no fat, no sugar, and negligible sodium, posing minimal direct cardiovascular risk per serving; moderate caffeine intake (~34 mg per can) is generally well-tolerated in cardiovascular patients, but individuals with arrhythmias or caffeine sensitivity should limit intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coke Zero (officially Coca-Cola Zero Sugar) is a globally distributed zero-calorie, zero-sugar carbonated cola beverage produced by The Coca-Cola Company. It is sweetened with a blend of aspartame and acesulfame potassium (Ace-K) instead of sucrose or high-fructose corn syrup, delivering a cola flavour profile closely resembling regular Coca-Cola without contributing glycemic carbohydrates or calories. The beverage is carbonated, dark brown in colour, and has a crisp, slightly sweet, mildly bitter taste with characteristic cola aromatics derived from natural flavours and caramel colour. In the Singapore food-service context, Coke Zero is commonly offered as a beverage pairing at Korean fried chicken restaurants, where it complements the rich, savoury, and often spicy flavour profile of Korean fried chicken dishes. Nutritionally, a 330 ml can provides approximately 0 kcal, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 0 g carbohydrate, and approximately 20 mg sodium. The phosphoric acid content contributes to its slightly acidic pH. Clinically, Coke Zero is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or caloric restriction, as it does not raise blood glucose. However, its phosphoric acid content may affect dental enamel with frequent consumption, and its caffeine content (~34 mg per 330 ml) warrants moderation in caffeine-sensitive individuals. It contains phenylalanine (from aspartame) and is contraindicated for individuals with phenylketonuria (PKU).

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC 174851) for zero-sugar cola with caffeine, corroborated by USDA Branded Foods entry for Coca-Cola Zero Sugar (FDC 2346009). The zero-calorie, zero-macronutrient profile is consistent across multiple approved sources. GI assignment follows peer-reviewed convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages.
notes: energy_kcal is confirmed zero: this is a zero-calorie beverage by formulation; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. protein_g is confirmed zero: contains no protein by product formulation; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. fat_g is confirmed zero: contains no fat by product formulation; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero: contains no glycemic carbohydrates; sweetened exclusively with non-nutritive sweeteners (aspartame and acesulfame-K); confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. sugar_g is confirmed zero by formulation. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero by formulation. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are confirmed zero by formulation per USDA source. All vitamin fields reported as 0.0 are confirmed zero per USDA FDC SR Legacy; this beverage contains no meaningful micronutrient content. iron_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, vitamin_c_mg, and all B-vitamins are 0.0 as confirmed zero per USDA FDC. Phosphorus (13 mg/100 g) is present due to phosphoric acid. Water content 99.7 g/100 g per USDA FDC. Sodium 6 mg/100 g (~20 mg per 330 ml can). Contains phenylalanine from aspartame — contraindicated in phenylketonuria (PKU). The food_name in the input ('CB2. Coke Zero') is a menu-style label; canonical name normalised to 'Coke Zero (Coca-Cola Zero Sugar)'. Cuisine tag 'Korean' is retained from the field survey source context (served at a Korean restaurant) but the product itself is a globally manufactured beverage of US origin. The Singaporean context (served in Singapore) triggers the requirement for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names and aliases.
